response.justification: Currently Staff Engineer at Naver working on AI-agent infrastructure and agentic workflows—directly aligned with the role's AI-native environment and expectations. Brings deep distributed systems expertise (Dunnhumby: millions of requests/second), strong TypeScript, proven ownership mindset, and mentorship track record. However, Python is listed as a must-have skill and is completely absent from the resume, operational readiness and on-call experience are not demonstrated, and tenure at current role is only ~one year. Interview should focus on Python readiness and operational background before advancing.
strengths: Currently Staff Engineer at AI-native company (Naver) working on agentic systems infrastructure and MCP frameworks—already performing the target role
Deep distributed systems expertise from Dunnhumby (millions of requests/second) with hands-on low-latency optimization, async I/O, and high-throughput database skills
Strong systems thinking across multiple domains, consistent focus on software craftsmanship (TDD, CI/CD, refactoring), and demonstrated mentorship through teaching and open-source projects
concerns: Python is listed as must-have skill but does not appear anywhere in resume or skills list, despite being critical for infrastructure work at this level
Operational readiness, incident response capability, and out-of-hours on-call experience not mentioned or demonstrated on resume
Agile/sprint methodology experience and stakeholder management not explicitly mentioned; joined Naver only one year ago (April 2025), limiting clarity on stability

key_factors.factor: Already Staff Engineer in AI-native environment with agentic systems experience
Proven distributed systems expertise at high-throughput scale
